items.title: Gilmour ruled out of World Cup - could Man Utd's Fletcher replace him?
items.summary: Scotland midfielder Billy Gilmour is ruled out of the World Cup after picking up a knee injury during Saturday's 4-1 friendly win over Curacao.

items.title: Man City legend Kevin De Bruyne and stunning wife Michele soak up the sun on luxury holiday to Ibiza
items.summary: KEVIN DE BRUYNE and his stunning wife Michele Lacroix looked the perfect couple as they enjoy a holiday in Ibiza. items.title: ‘They’ve been hard done by’ – Arsenal denied extra-time penalty vs PSG leaving Declan Rice and Mikel Arteta raging
items.summary: ARSENAL fans leapt up from their seats across the world after Noni Madueke was denied a penalty after he folded to the floor in the box. items.title: Bukayo Saka’s fiancee puts on busty display in low-cut top as she cheers on Arsenal star at Champions League final

items.title: Arsenal suffer penalty heartbreak as PSG win Champions League final
items.summary: Arsenal suffered heartbreak in the Champions League final in Budapest as they were beaten 4-3 on penalties by PSG after a tense 1-1 draw in 120 minutes.

items.title: 'Heartbreak for Arsenal' | Fans react as Gunners lose on penalties to PSG
items.summary: Gabriel missed Arsenal's fifth penalty as PSG celebrated winning the Champions League in successive years.

items.title: How does Iraola's style of play compare to Klopp and Slot?
items.summary: With Andoni Iraola being linked with the Liverpool job, Sky Sports News' Mark McAdam analyses how his style of play compares to Jurgen Klopp and Arne Slot.

items.title: PSG go back-to-back and join 'greatest of all-time'
items.summary: Paris St-Germain underline their status as one of European football's greatest ever teams by retaining their Champions League crown with victory against Arsenal.

items.title: PSG retain Champions League after penalty shootout win over Arsenal
items.summary: Arsenal lose a penalty shootout as Paris St-Germain win the Champions League for a second successive year.

items.title: Gabriel blazes penalty over as PSG win Champions League
items.summary: Paris St-Germain retain their Champions League crown as Arsenal defender Gabriel misses the decisive spot-kick in a penalty shootout.

items.title: Dembele equalises from penalty spot for PSG
items.summary: PSG's Ousmane Dembele scores from the penalty spot to level the Champions League final against Arsenal in Budapest.

items.summary: Kai Havertz fires Arsenal into an early lead in the Champions League final against Paris St-Germain in Budapest.

items.summary: It was a showpiece that held the football world in its grip, the tension mounting exponentially, everything on the line. items.title: Distraught Gabriel consoled by PSG star immediately after Arsenal star’s penalty miss in Champions League shootout
items.summary: CRESTFALLEN Gabriel fell into the arms of Paris Saint-Germain star Marquinhos after missing the penalty that lost Arsenal the Champions League. items.title: PSG 1 Arsenal 1 (4-3 on pens): Gunners suffer Champions League final penalty shootout heartbreak to end Double dream
items.summary: THAT’S not fair. Simply not fair. Yes, you can argue that PSG deserved to triumph on a night of sheer drama. items.summary: KHVICHA KVARATSKHELIA showed off a nasty looking leg injury after he was fouled by Cristhian Mosquera. items.summary: FOOTBALL fans thought Eva Murati was in a league of her own as she attended Saturday's Budapest final. items.title: Arne Slot leaves Liverpool with immediate effect
items.summary: Liverpool have confirmed that head coach Arne Slot will leave the club with immediate effect.

items.title: Barcelona sign Gordon from Newcastle for $82M
items.summary: Barcelona have announced the signing of England forward Anthony Gordon from Newcastle United on a five-year contract.

items.title: Scotland injury blow as Gilmour ruled out of World Cup
items.summary: Scotland midfielder Billy Gilmour has been ruled out of the World Cup after suffering a knee injury in the win over Curacao.

items.title: Peet tells Wigan to enjoy Challenge Cup celebrations - 'go as hard as you can'
items.summary: After clinching the Challenge Cup with a 40-10 drubbing of Hull KR, Wigan Warriors head coach Matt Peet had one message for his side on how to celebrate: "Go as hard as you can for as long as you can".

items.summary: In the early stages, Scotland looked to be opting for something different. That is, heading to a major tournament while completely unburdened by expectation. items.title: Ex-footballer linked to top club, 29, arrested after friend’s body found ‘beheaded and burnt in suitcase’
items.summary: AN EX-footballer has been arrested after his friend's beheaded body was found burnt in a suitcase. The victim's charred remains were discovered after firefighters were called to a grass fire in Curacavi, Chile, on April 12. items.title: Reports: Sterling arrested; suspected drug driving...
items.summary: Raheem Sterling was arrested on Thursday morning on suspicion of driving "whilst unfit through drugs," according to reports.

items.title: Injured Davies headlines Canada's WCup roster
items.summary: Canada captain and Bayern Munich star Alphonso Davies was named to Jesse Marsch's final 26-man World Cup squad despite carrying a hamstring injury that could sideline him for the tournament opener.

items.title: PSG beat Arsenal in shootout to retain UCL title
items.summary: Paris Saint-Germain became just the second team to win back-to-back Champions League titles after rallying to beat Arsenal following a penalty shootout in Budapest, Hungary, on Saturday.

items.title: Iraola the frontrunner to become Liverpool boss
items.summary: Andoni Iraola is the leading candidate to succeed Arne Slot as Liverpool head coach, sources have told ESPN.
